So what is the gatekeeper? When you're blaming, criticizing, labeling other people, hating, look, you're not hustling. You can't hustle and hate at the same time. It's just impossible. When I'm on my full hustle, I'm jamming and working. I'm not criticizing anybody. I don't have time. I'm focused on where I'm going and what I'm doing. So the moment, the moment you start criticizing, hating, condemning, labeling, making other people responsible for something that's not happening for you, you need to know this. You're just not hustling enough. This person, by the way, this person, this gatekeeper, this person is put in charge of protecting me, the executive, the company owner, is doing their job. And they're actually doing their job better than you're doing your job. See, they're not talking about how terrible a person you are. The gatekeeper's job is not to prevent you from getting to the decision maker. The decision maker doesn't need flakes and doesn't need to waste their time. So the gatekeeper, this person, this sitting between you is trying to make sure you're not somewhat. Not just to waste your time. They're trying to prevent something that's happened in the past from happening again. Understand there was a time when there was no gatekeeper. ## [03:02 - 04:56]

Come in for a picture, come in for a handshake, come in for a meeting. You want some coaching. You want to sit down. I'll always say yes to you. But I got Sherry and I got Jared and I got Todd and they're like, no, no, dude, we got him on other programs and other projects. It's not going to happen. Rule number one about the gatekeeper. They are not keeping you away from your goals and dreams. You, on the other hand, are keeping you away from your goals and dreams. They are trying to prevent people from wasting the time of the God, the king, the decision maker. Number two, the harder it is to get to a decision maker, the easier they are to close. In most cases, the people in my office, the gatekeepers, there's a bunch of them, are protecting me from myself more than they are trying to keep you away. Gatekeepers protect the people in the organization that have power to pull strings or that influence that power. Okay, from what? From wasting time, from hearing pitches, from charity cases, from getting involved in situations that just can't go anywhere. The goal is so that the important people can do the important things.
